subtitles: Strategic rationale, deal terms, integration, and path to close
Frame the strategic rationale via executive voices
Show how Resy+Tock+TheFork form a closed-loop dining strategy
Connect dining to AmEx broader experiences-led growth thesis
context_bands: Show how Resy+Tock+TheFork form a closed-loop dining strategy. To put a fine point on it: TheFork is the European pillar of a Resy/Tock/TheFork ecosystem.
Connect dining to AmEx broader experiences-led growth thesis. To put a fine point on it: Dining is the highest-frequency premium-engagement category.
Headline deal terms. To put a fine point on it: AmEx buys TheFork for $700M cash, closing by end 2026.
Revenue, EBITDA, growth rate. To put a fine point on it: $232M revenue, $28M EBITDA, +25% YoY growth.
Geographic + venue scale. To put a fine point on it: 50,000+ restaurants across 11 European countries; 75,000 total venues post-deal.
Deal process timeline. To put a fine point on it: Put-option agreement signed; closing expected before end 2026.
How AmEx will integrate TheFork. To put a fine point on it: TheFork continues under existing leadership; Goldman/Goodwin/Reed Smith advising.
How Resy/Tock/TheFork combine. To put a fine point on it: Three platforms combine into the largest premium dining network.
Third-party validation of strategic fit. To put a fine point on it: William Blair: deal consistent with AmEx premium-experiences focus.
What must happen to close. To put a fine point on it: Labor consultation + regulatory approvals; close before end 2026.
Risk factors around closing. To put a fine point on it: Consultation, regulatory, and execution risks could delay or prevent close.
Company history context. To put a fine point on it: Founded 2007; 420M guests sent to restaurants in 15 years.
People + advisors context. To put a fine point on it: Ambeskovic CEO; Goldman/Goodwin/Reed Smith; BTIG flags Viator concern.
so_whats: This means AmEx sees dining as core to brand engagement; TheFork extends that into Europe.
That puts TheFork at the center: TheFork is the European pillar of a Resy/Tock/TheFork ecosystem.
This means Dining is the highest-frequency premium-engagement category.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: AmEx buys TheFork for $700M cash, closing by end 2026.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: $232M revenue, $28M EBITDA, +25% YoY growth.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: 50,000+ restaurants across 11 European countries; 75,000 total venues post-deal.
The implication is the clock begins now: Put-option agreement signed; closing expected before end 2026.
This means TheFork continues under existing leadership; Goldman/Goodwin/Reed Smith advising.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: Three platforms combine into the largest premium dining network.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: William Blair: deal consistent with AmEx premium-experiences focus.
The implication is clear: Labor consultation + regulatory approvals; close before end 2026.
The implication is clear: Consultation, regulatory, and execution risks could delay or prevent close.
This means the numbers underpin the thesis: Founded 2007; 420M guests sent to restaurants in 15 years.
This means Ambeskovic CEO; Goldman/Goodwin/Reed Smith; BTIG flags Viator concern.
